label: Economic rate of return benchmarks for World Bank project appraisal

err_benchmark.label: Minimum economic rate of return (ERR) threshold
err_benchmark.note: Projects generally require ERR > 6% to be considered for financing. This is a hurdle rate, not a social discount rate in the Green Book sense.

sensitivity.note: ERR threshold varies by country risk, sector, and project type. Low-risk infrastructure in stable MICs may use 4-6%. High-risk projects in fragile states may require 10%+.

country_risk_adjustment.description: The World Bank does not prescribe a universal social discount rate. The ERR benchmark reflects the opportunity cost of capital in the borrowing country. Higher rates for countries with more productive alternative uses of capital.

low_income.note: Higher opportunity cost of scarce capital

notes: The World Bank uses the Economic Rate of Return (ERR) as the primary project selection metric, not NPV or BCR. A project passes the ERR test if ERR > opportunity cost of capital. This is conceptually different from the UK/EU/US approach of discounting at a fixed social rate and computing NPV.

source.publication: Guidelines for the Economic Analysis of Projects (2017); World Bank Operational Policy OP 10.04
